records.display_name: Concept development guidelines

payload.claim_text: Concept development guidelines: focus on improvements that can be implemented quickly and that do not adversely affect safety or traffic flow, build on left-turn lane improvement projects being implemented starting 2011, no roadway widening unless significant benefits are likely, maintain on-street parking and deliveries for businesses
payload.description: Guidelines for developing the corridor concept plan

records.raw_text: Concept Development Guidelines - Focus on quick improvements, Build on left-turn lane projects, No roadway widening, Maintain parking and deliveries

records.display_name: Concerns from CAC and public meeting 2010-2011

payload.claim_text: Community concerns raised in 2010-11 include: reduce congestion, improve safety for drivers and pedestrians, enforce traffic regulations more aggressively, buses are too slow, maintain on-street parking and delivery access for businesses, provide more time for pedestrians to cross Hylan Boulevard, provide sidewalks where none exist
payload.description: Issues raised by CAC (Community Advisory Committee) and public meeting attendees

records.raw_text: Concerns from CAC and Public Meeting Issues Raised in 2010-11 - Reduce congestion, Improve safety, Buses too slow, Maintain parking, More crossing time, Sidewalks

records.display_name: Causes of bus passenger delays on Hylan corridor

payload.claim_text: Causes of bus passenger delays along Hylan Boulevard: traffic congestion, frequent express bus stops, avoiding stopped buses, avoiding parked cars, narrow lanes, paying fare on bus
payload.description: Causes of bus passenger delays on the Hylan Boulevard corridor

records.raw_text: Causes: Traffic congestion, Frequent express bus stops, Avoiding stopped buses, Avoiding parked cars, Narrow lanes, Paying fare on bus

records.display_name: Project goals for Hylan Boulevard study

payload.claim_text: Project Goals: Improve traffic flow, reduce bus travel time and improve reliability, improve transit access to Manhattan, Brooklyn and to Staten Island destinations, improve safety for all corridor users
payload.description: Project goals of the Hylan Boulevard Transportation Improvement Study

records.raw_text: Improve traffic flow, Reduce bus travel time and improve reliability, Improve transit access to Manhattan, Brooklyn and to Staten Island destinations, Improve safety for all corridor users

records.display_name: Ongoing improvements along Hylan Boulevard corridor

payload.claim_text: Ongoing improvements along the Hylan Boulevard corridor include: left-turn bays, senior safety pedestrian refuge islands, Hylan/New Dorp reconstruction, Hylan/Steuben signal improvements, Amboy Road congested corridor study, countdown pedestrian signals, extension of S78 to Bricktown Mall, ten added peak hour Hylan Boulevard corridor express bus trips in 2011
payload.description: Improvements already being implemented along the corridor

records.raw_text: Ongoing Improvements: Left-Turn Bays, Pedestrian Refuge Islands, Hylan/New Dorp Reconstruction, Signal Improvements, Amboy Road Study, Countdown Signals, S78 extension, express bus trips

records.display_name: Traffic conditions AM and PM on Hylan corridor

payload.claim_text: A.M.: Congestion occurs primarily northbound between Guyon and Seaver Avenues. Most other intersections operate well. Traffic flows better southbound. P.M.: Congestion is heaviest at New Dorp Lane and Tysens Lane. Most other intersections operate well. Traffic flows better northbound.
payload.description: Existing traffic conditions along Hylan Boulevard during AM and PM peak periods

records.raw_text: AM: Congestion northbound between Guyon and Seaver Avenues. PM: Congestion heaviest at New Dorp Lane and Tysens Lane.

records.display_name: Hylan Boulevard

description: BRT corridor in Staten Island
Hylan Boulevard is the only continuous North-South route on the south shore of Staten Island. Study corridor extends from Richmond Avenue area to the Verrazano-Narrows Bridge area.
Hylan Boulevard corridor from Steuben Street to Richmond Avenue, Staten Island
Continuous North-South route on the south shore of Staten Island; corridor for Hylan Boulevard Transportation Improvement Study and proposed Select Bus Service.
Study corridor along Hylan Boulevard and Richmond Avenue on Staten Island, from the south (Richmond Avenue area) to Old Town Road
Study corridor for Hylan Boulevard Transportation Improvement Study in Staten Island
Bus lanes on Hylan Boulevard between Lincoln Avenue and Steuben Street
Corridor for Hylan Boulevard Transportation Improvement Study with S79 SBS improvements

One of the most important transportation corridors on Staten Island, serving as the primary route along Staten Island's east and south shores. Carries local bus services connecting to the Staten Island Ferry and to Brooklyn, and express bus services to Manhattan.
Difficult Trip corridor; Phase I BRT planning for SBS route connecting Staten Island south shore to Brooklyn
S79 SBS corridor.
Received attention at all Staten Island workshop tables; some participants felt a BRT route was unnecessary because of recent improvements to traffic signal timing

payload.description: BRT corridor in Staten Island
payload.limits: Between Lincoln Avenue and Steuben Street
